title: Thelypteris stokesii
taxonomy: Thelypteris stokesii is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, which is part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. This family is often associated with the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all vascular plants that produce spores. The classification of Thelypteris stokesii further breaks down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. The genus Thelypteris is typically grouped within the family Thelypteridaceae, characterized by the presence of sterile leaves. This classification allows scientists to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Thelypteris stokesii in relation to other fern species.
uses: Thelypteris stokesii is reportedly used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Caribbean, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is sometimes used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin issues. Additionally, it is thought to have antimicrobial properties, which could be beneficial in wound healing. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal uses and potential side effects. More studies are required to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.

properties: Thelypteris stokesii, also known as Stokes' fern, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine.
